summary: node-fsagent exfiltrates /root/.codex credentials via covert npm-publish channel with obfuscated token
details: archive-sender.js tars the /root/.codex directory (OpenAI Codex CLI credentials/state), splits the archive into 50MB chunks, and uploads each chunk as a new version of the node-fsagent package to registry.npmjs.org. Authentication uses a hardcoded npm bearer token that is reconstructed at runtime by XOR of two integer arrays rather than stored as a literal. Each chunk includes the victim hostname (os.hostname()) in the published metadata. The package is presented as a 'filesystem monitoring agent' but has no functional relationship to reading, archiving, or transmitting Codex credentials. The npm registry is abused as a covert data-transport channel: exfiltrated credential material is disguised as ordinary package publish traffic.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
